# Feature: proof-jest

> Requires: schema_proof_file
> Scope: scripts/proof/jest_purlin.js

## What it does

A Jest custom reporter that parses proof markers from test names and emits feature-scoped proof JSON files. Test names must contain `[proof:feature:PROOF-N:RULE-N:tier]` for the reporter to collect them.

## Rules

- RULE-1: The reporter MUST parse proof markers matching `[proof:<feature>:<PROOF-N>:<RULE-N>:<tier>]` from test titles
- RULE-2: The tier field MUST default to "default" when omitted from the marker
- RULE-3: The reporter MUST map Jest's "passed" status to "pass" and all other statuses to "fail"
- RULE-4: The reporter MUST write proof files to the same directory as the matching spec, using `<feature>.proofs-<tier>.json` naming
- RULE-5: The reporter MUST use feature-scoped overwrite — purge existing entries for the tested feature, keep entries from other features

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): A test named `"does X [proof:feat:PROOF-1:RULE-1:default]"` produces a proof entry with feature "feat", id "PROOF-1", rule "RULE-1"
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): A marker `[proof:feat:PROOF-1:RULE-1]` without tier produces tier "default"
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): A passing Jest test produces status "pass"; a failing test produces status "fail"
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): The proof file is written at `specs/<category>/<feature>.proofs-default.json`
- PROOF-5 (RULE-5): Running tests for feature A preserves existing entries for feature B in the same proof file
